diabetes.dataset_description: UCI 130-US Hospitals Diabetes dataset. Predicts hospital readmission for diabetic patients. Contains many categorical medical codes (admission_type_id, discharge_disposition_id, admission_source_id) and medication columns with values like 'No', 'Up', 'Down', 'Steady'. Leakage columns encounter_id and patient_nbr already removed.
must_do: encode_categorical with ordinal for tree-based efficiency
diabetes.notes: Dataset domain: medical/clinical. Categorical nature: mix of nominal (race, gender) and ordinal-like (medication dosage changes: No/Steady/Up/Down). High-cardinality columns: diag_1, diag_2, diag_3 (ICD codes, very high cardinality). ML model encoding guidance: Ordinal encoding is efficient and appropriate. Trees make binary splits at thresholds, so ordinal values work well regardless of whether categories have a natural order. This also reduces dimensionality compared to one-hot, which can improve performance with high-cardinality features.
